fact: November 28 is exactly 333 days into the year! That's three 3s in a row - like nature's secret code.
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely bonkers burying their last acorns before winter hits hard.
The sun sets before 5 PM in most of Canada and northern US states around this date - dinner time happens in total darkness!
Down in Antarctica, penguin chicks are hatching right now while their parents slide around on ice that never melts.
Maple trees in cold regions have completely shut down their food-making by this date - they're basically taking a 4-month nap.
Why do cats get extra fluffy around November 28? Their winter coats are at peak thickness to trap warm air like tiny fur balloons.
The moon looks extra bright and crisp on cold November nights because there's less water vapor in the air to blur it.
In northern Alaska, the sun barely peeks over the horizon for just 3 hours around this date - like a giant game of peek-a-boo.
Deer have grown their thickest, warmest coats by late November, making them look like walking fuzzy footballs.
November 28 falls smack in the middle of Sagittarius season - when the archer constellation shoots across the night sky.
Ocean water around this date is actually warmer than the air in coastal areas, which is why you see steam rising from lakes and rivers.
Evergreen trees like pine and spruce are secretly growing right now - their roots keep working underground even when it's freezing above.
